title: sw-dev-blueprint task plan (tasks/plan.json)
description: The EM's decomposition of the frozen ERD into atomic one-file tasks. This artifact is the EM's ONLY channel of authority: the shell orchestrator validates it with scripts/validate-plan.py before any coder runs, walks it in topological order, and owns all status/completion state itself. There is deliberately NO status field — the EM never marks anything done (D-26). Cross-file semantic checks (unique files, ERD-inventory coverage, exactly-once test mapping, acyclic DAG, contract-id existence) live in validate-plan.py, not in this schema.

version.description: Plan revision counter. The EM bumps this on every re-emit (decomposition_wrong revisions).

erd_version.description: The scripts/.approved/VERSION this plan was derived from. A plan whose erd_version lags the frozen VERSION is stale and fails validation.

id.description: Task id, unique within the plan.

file.description: The ONE file this task creates or modifies, relative to repo root, under the build lane (.gate-paths build=). Unique across tasks — one task per file, one file per task. phase-gate.sh task enforces that the coder touched exactly this path.

depends_on.description: Task ids that must be done before this one. Must form a DAG.

brief.description: Self-contained coder brief per BLUEPRINT.md Rule 8: exact path, exact signatures, exact inputs/outputs, exact acceptance condition. Zero inference gaps — the coder sees this brief and nothing else.

contracts.description: Ids of frozen contract entries (from scripts/.approved/contracts.json) this task implements or touches. Every id must exist in the frozen contracts.

tests.description: Frozen TPM test node-ids expected to pass once this task and its dependencies are done. This is the oracle PROJECTION (D-28): the EM schedules the TPM's tests onto tasks; it never authors acceptance. Across the whole plan, every frozen node-id must appear exactly once.

smoke_check.description: OPTIONAL shell command for tasks with no covering frozen test (internal plumbing). Explicitly non-oracular: a passing smoke_check proves the file loads/runs, never that the feature is correct.
